title: Rizong Monastery
tagline: Rizong Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Rizong (or Rhizong) gompa, Gelugpa or Yellow Hat Buddhist monastery, also called the Yuma Changchubling, is in Ladakh, India.
overview.description: Rizong (or Rhizong) gompa, Gelugpa or Yellow Hat Buddhist monastery, also called the Yuma Changchubling, is in Ladakh, India. It is situated at the top of a rocky side valley on the north side of the Indus, to the west of Alchi on the way to Lamayuru. It was established in 1831 by Lama Tsultim Nima under the Gelukpa order, at Ri-rdzong. There are 40 monks in the monastery.

topPlaces.name: Giramangu
topPlaces.description: Giramangu, also known as Gaira Mangue or Mangyu, is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Saspol Caves
topPlaces.description: Saspol Caves (a.k.a. Gon-Nila-Phuk Cave Temples) are situated in the hills behind Suspol, Ladakh, India, which is about 76 km from the city centre of Leh.

topPlaces.name: Hemis Shukpachan
topPlaces.description: Hemis-Shukpachan or Hemishok Pachan is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located on the right side bank of Indus River and is in the Likir tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Mangyu temple complex
topPlaces.description: The Mangyu temple complex located in the village of Mangyu, Ladakh is one of the earliest in Ladakh, India. Believed to be contemporaneous to the temples at Alchi Monastery and Sumda Chun, the earliest structures are supposed to be dating to the late 12th/early 13th century but as per the oral history and local belief the temples were established by Lotsawa Rinchen Zangpo.

topPlaces.name: Alchi
topPlaces.description: Alchi is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, on the banks of the Indus River 70 km (43.49miles) downstream from the capital Leh.

topPlaces.name: Saspochey
topPlaces.description: Saspochey is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Alchi Monastery
topPlaces.description: Alchi Monastery (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་ཆོས་འཁོར།) or Alchi Gompa (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་དགོམ་པ།, also Alci) is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery, known more as a monastic complex (chos-'khor) of temples in Alchi village in the Leh District, under the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council of the Union Territory of Ladakh, India. The complex comprises four separate settlements in the Alchi village in the lower Ladakh region with monuments dated to different periods.

topPlaces.name: Nimoo Bazgo Hydroelectric Plant
topPlaces.description: The Nimoo Bazgo Power Project is a run-of-the-river power project on the Indus River situated at Alchi village, 75 kilometres (47 mi) from Leh in the Indian Union Territory of Ladakh. The project was conceived on 1 July 2001 and approved on 8 June 2005, and construction began on 23rd Sept, 2006.

items.note: Giramangu, also known as Gaira Mangue or Mangyu, is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.
Saspol Caves (a.k.a. Gon-Nila-Phuk Cave Temples) are situated in the hills behind Suspol, Ladakh, India, which is about 76 km from the city centre of Leh.
Hemis-Shukpachan or Hemishok Pachan is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located on the right side bank of Indus River and is in the Likir tehsil.

The Mangyu temple complex located in the village of Mangyu, Ladakh is one of the earliest in Ladakh, India. Believed to be contemporaneous to the temples at Alchi Monastery and Sumda Chun, the earliest structures are supposed to be dating to the late 12th/early 13th century but as per the oral history and local belief the temples were established by Lotsawa Rinchen Zangpo.
Alchi is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil, on the banks of the Indus River 70 km (43.49miles) downstream from the capital Leh.
Saspochey is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Likir tehsil.

Alchi Monastery (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་ཆོས་འཁོར།) or Alchi Gompa (Tibetan: ཨ་ལྕི་དགོམ་པ།, also Alci) is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery, known more as a monastic complex (chos-'khor) of temples in Alchi village in the Leh District, under the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council of the Union Territory of Ladakh, India. The complex comprises four separate settlements in the Alchi village in the lower Ladakh region with monuments dated to different periods.

The Nimoo Bazgo Power Project is a run-of-the-river power project on the Indus River situated at Alchi village, 75 kilometres (47 mi) from Leh in the Indian Union Territory of Ladakh. The project was conceived on 1 July 2001 and approved on 8 June 2005, and construction began on 23rd Sept, 2006.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rizong Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rizong Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rizong Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (245 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rizong Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rizong Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rizong Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Rizong Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rizong Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Giramangu, Saspol Caves, Hemis Shukpachan, Mangyu temple complex, Alchi.
